data.title: McCoy Global's Q2 2025: Revenue up 21% at $24.1M, net earnings down 56% at $1.4M. Declares $0.025 dividend per share.
data.description: McCoy Global Inc. reported its financial results for Q2 2025, including a 21% increase in revenue to $24.1 million from $19.9 million in Q2 2024. The smartProduct revenue accounted for 58% of total revenue at $13.9 million, up 117% from the previous period. Net earnings decreased by 56% to $1.4 million due to higher stock-based compensation expenses. Adjusted EBITDA was $4.8 million. McCoy made advancements in its Digital Technology Roadmap, securing $11.0 million in contract awards for its smarTR™ system. The corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.025 per common share. Despite challenges, McCoy's CEO Jim Rakievich remains confident in their long-term trajectory. The company's Vice President & CFO Lindsay McGill noted a growing smartProduct adoption and a total backlog of $24.6 million as of June 30, 2025. Total revenue for H1 2025 was $43.4 million. SmartProduct revenue increased by 119% compared to 2024, accounting for 58% of total revenue. McCoy's gross profit margin improved to 36% for the quarter. G&A expenses were $4.3 million for the quarter and accounted for $7.6 million year-to-date, affected by stock-based compensation and bad debts provisions. Sales and marketing expenses increased due to expanded technical salesforce and marketing efforts.

data.title: Big Rock Brewery Inc. reports 45% increase in second quarter sales volumes, with net revenue up to $16.6 million.
data.description: Big Rock Brewery Inc. reported significant growth in sales volume for the trailing twelve months, with a 39% increase. In their financial results for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024, the corporation saw total sales volumes up 45.3% to 84,116 hl, with net revenue increasing by 34.2% to $16.6 million. Gross margin increased to $5.2 million, and operating income improved to $0.4 million from a loss in the previous year. Net income also increased to $0.3 million, and Adjusted EBITDA rose to $1.0 million. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024, total sales volumes increased by 47.9% to 152,460 hl, with net revenue up 32.7% to $27.8 million. Gross margin improved to $8.9 million, operating income increased, and net income saw a significant rise to $0.2 million from a loss in the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA also showed a substantial increase to $1.7 million. Sales volumes, net revenue, and other financial figures for both three and six-month periods are detailed in the report. Full details of the story are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: FiscalNote will attend Oppenheimer's 28th Annual Tech, Internet & Comms Conf in Washington.
data.description: FiscalNote will be attending the Oppenheimer 28th Annual Technology, Internet & Communications Conference in Washington.

data.title: KKR invests in Med-Metrix with Harvest Partners, supporting RCM solutions. Important numbers: KKR, Harvest, Med-Metrix.
data.description: A prominent global investment firm, KKR, disclosed that their credit funds and accounts played a primary role in providing financing to back the growth investment made by Harvest Partners, LP-managed funds in Med-Metrix, LLC, a prominent provider of tech-driven Revenue Cycle Management solutions. KKR Capital Markets also participated as the Left Lead Arranger and Bookrunner for the deal. The Company's manнуюagements plans for the funds weren't specified, but the figures weren't mentioned.

data.title: Sylvamo (NYSE: SLVM) reports Q2 2025 earnings in line with outlook despite $13 million forex impact. Chairman remains optimistic for stronger second half.
data.description: Sylvamo (NYSE: SLVM), the global paper company, has announced its second quarter 2025 earnings. The company overcame a $13 million negative foreign exchange impact and successfully managed a significant planned maintenance outage quarter in over five years. Jean-Michel Ribiéras, Chairman and CEO, stated that the second quarter earnings met their expectations. An audio webcast discussing the earnings will be hosted at 10 a.m. EDT on investors.sylvamo.com.

data.title: Capital City Fruit enhances traceability with ReposiTrak network for suppliers and retail customers.
data.description: Capital City Fruit, a prominent player in the fresh produce industry, has recently partnered with the ReposiTrak Traceability Network® to enhance its food traceability program. This collaboration will facilitate the intake of traceability data from Capital City Fruit's suppliers and enable the sharing of such data with the company’s retail customers. The implementation of this new system underscores the company's commitment to ensuring food safety and quality throughout its supply chain. This strategic move showcases Capital City Fruit's dedication to fulfilling its responsibilities as a distributor.

data.title: Canopy Growth Q1 FY2026: 24% increase in cannabis revenue, strong top line growth in Canada adult-use business.
data.description: In Smiths Falls, Ontario, Canopy Growth Corporation (TSX:WEED) (Nasdaq: CGC) revealed its financial outcomes for the quarter concluded on June 30, 2025 ("Q1 FY2026"). The company reported its financial achievements in Canadian dollars. They experienced significant revenue growth in Q1 FY2026, primarily fueled by success in the Canadian adult-use cannabis sector. Canopy Growth is increasing its market share in the fast-growing segment.

data.title: CEO of Havoc AI, Paul Lwin, joins WP Intelligence Council for maritime autonomy. Aug. 8, 2025 /PRNewswire.
data.description: Havoc AI, a prominent player in maritime autonomy based in Providence, R.I., made a significant announcement on August 8, 2025. The Co-Founder and CEO, Paul Lwin, has been appointed to the WP Intelligence Council by the Washington Post. This council offers a fresh leadership avenue for Lwin as he continues to steer Havoc AI's groundbreaking initiatives in the industry.

data.title: Wendy's Q2 2025: Global sales $3.7B (-1.8%), Int'l sales +8.7%, 26 new restaurants added, on track for 2-3% net unit growth.
data.description: The worldwide sales for the company reached $3.7 billion, marking a slight decline of 1.8% compared to the previous period. On the international front, there was a significant growth of 8.7% in systemwide sales, observed across all regions. The company successfully added 26 new restaurants during this time frame and is progressing as planned to achieve a net growth in units ranging between 2% to 3% for the entire fiscal year. The reported diluted earnings per share, along with adjusted metrics, demonstrate positive performance and indicate a stable financial outlook for the company.

data.title: BrainStorm will announce Q2 financial results on August 14, 2025, at 8:30 AM Eastern Time. Leading developer of adult stem cell therapeutics for neurodegenerative diseases.
data.description: BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics Inc., a prominent developer of adult stem cell therapeutics for neurodegenerative diseases, has scheduled a conference call for August 14, 2025, at 8:30 AM Eastern Time. The call will be held by the company, known by its ticker symbol OTCQB: BCLI. The announcement was made on August 8, 2025, in New York.

data.title: Calumet reports a Q2 2025 net loss of $147.9 million and an adjusted EBITDA of $76.5 million. Cost reductions exceeding plan by $42 million.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, there was a reported net loss of $147.9 million, translating to a basic loss of $1.70 per common share. On a more positive note, the Adjusted EBITDA with Tax Attributes for the same period was $76.5 million. The company has been actively implementing cost-cutting measures, which have proven successful as they have already saved $42 million in operating expenses compared to the previous year.

data.title: Illumine Financial Partners joins MassMutual SC for expanded financial planning capabilities. Led by David Horine and Andrew Schmidt.
data.description: In a recent development on August 8, 2025, MassMutual of South Carolina has announced a new addition to their team with Illumine Financial Partners, headed by David Horine, ChFC®, ChSNC® and Andrew Schmidt, joining forces. This financial planning practice based in Charleston will now be operating under MassMutual of South Carolina.

data.title: RBL LLC receives investment from Modi Ventures, with Sahir Ali joining RBL's board of directors. Venture creation talks planned.
data.description: A significant investment has been made to support RBL's goal of launching and expanding innovative startups. Sahir Ali, the founder and general partner of Modi Ventures, will be joining the RBL board of directors. Both Ali from Modi Ventures and Wotton, the managing partner at RBL, are set to discuss venture creation at the upcoming Canaccord Genuity Growth event. This partnership is set to drive growth and create opportunities in the venture creation space.
